meta.description: A minimalist, editorial-grade digital agency design system. High contrast, generous whitespace, bold typography, and restrained use of a single accent color. The aesthetic is premium, confident, and modern — inspired by luxury branding and architectural photography.
meta.design_philosophy: Less is more. Every element earns its place. Typography does the heavy lifting; color is used as punctuation, not decoration. The design trusts whitespace to create hierarchy and breathing room.

color_palette.brand_colors_source: Extracted from brand banner. Two core colors: a vibrant periwinkle-blue (#587DFE) and a deep navy (#2C337A), plus derived light tints.
color_palette.secondary_background_notes: Very faint blue-white tint — keeps the clean feel but subtly ties to the brand blue. Use for alternating sections or card backgrounds.
color_palette.tertiary_background_notes: Slightly more visible blue tint. Use for feature highlight sections, code blocks, or callout areas.
color_palette.primary_text_notes: Black — extracted from the banner dark text. Used instead of pure black for all headings and primary text. Creates a softer, more branded feel than #000.
color_palette.secondary_text_notes: A mid-tone navy-gray. For body text, descriptions, and supporting content.
color_palette.muted_text_notes: Lighter navy-lavender. For metadata, dates, captions, and tertiary information.
color_palette.accent_notes: The vibrant periwinkle-blue from the banner background. Primary accent for interactive elements, brand marks, links, and highlights.
color_palette.accent_light_notes: For subtle hover backgrounds, active states, or secondary highlights.
color_palette.accent_lighter_notes: For tag backgrounds, light badges, or selected states.
color_palette.accent_lightest_notes: Near-white blue tint for subtle section backgrounds, input focus rings, or notification backgrounds.
color_palette.navy_notes: The deep navy from the banner dark text. For high-emphasis text, dark UI elements, or inverted sections.
color_palette.accent_usage: More present than a typical minimal palette — use for brand icon/logo mark, interactive link underlines, quote marks, hover states, section accents, and occasional full background fills on CTA or hero sections. Still intentional, not everywhere.
color_palette.dividers_notes: Cool-tinted light gray that harmonizes with the blue palette.

inverted_section.notes: For CTA banners or hero sections. White text on brand blue — as shown in the banner.

inverted_section_dark.notes: Alternative inverted section using deep navy. More serious/authoritative tone.

typography.approach: Type-driven design. Headlines are the dominant visual element on every section. No decorative fonts — one family throughout with weight variation creating hierarchy.

font_stack.alternative_suggestion: Can substitute with 'Neue Haas Grotesk', 'Helvetica Neue', or 'Switzer' for a similar geometric sans-serif feel.

hero_headline.notes: Extremely large, tight leading. These headlines command attention and often span 2-3 lines. They use natural sentence casing, never all-caps.

body.notes: Body text is always in the muted/secondary color, never full black. This creates a clear visual hierarchy where headlines pop.

label.case: uppercase or sentence-case depending on context
label.notes: Used for metadata like dates, categories, section labels (e.g., '/BLOG/'). Always in muted_text color.

blockquote.notes: Large, impactful pull quotes. Accompanied by a large blue quotation mark icon positioned to the left.

spacing.philosophy: Extremely generous whitespace. Sections are separated by vast empty space that would feel 'too much' in conventional design but creates a premium, editorial feel here.
spacing.notes: Content never stretches full-width. Even on large screens, text blocks are constrained to ~640px wide. The remaining space is either empty or used for secondary content in a split layout.

grid_system.notes: Asymmetric layouts are preferred over centered content. Content often sits at the left third while the right two-thirds hold a different element (or vice versa).

split_layout.description: Content divided into two unequal columns. One side holds text (headline + body + CTA), the other holds imagery, a form, or a quote. Used for contact sections, about sections, and CTAs.
split_layout.alignment: Text column is top-aligned, not vertically centered.

hero_cta_section.description: A large bold headline with a short description and a single text-link CTA. Content is right-aligned or centered with massive surrounding whitespace.

blog_grid.description: Featured post takes up the left half as a large card with full-bleed image. Two smaller cards stack vertically on the right. Below, additional articles are listed in a horizontal row format.
blog_grid.featured_card_ratio: roughly 50% width, large image with title below
blog_grid.secondary_cards: smaller, equal-sized, with image and title below
blog_grid.list_format: horizontal row: thumbnail (small square) | date | title

article_list.description: Clean horizontal rows with small square thumbnail on the left, date in the middle, and title on the right. Separated by subtle horizontal dividers.
article_list.thumbnail_size: approximately 80px x 80px, with subtle rounded corners (4px)

quote_section.description: Full-width section with a large blockquote. Blue quotation mark icon on the left, quote text on the right. Attribution below with circular avatar photo, name, and title.
quote_section.attribution_style: Name in bold (600), title in muted text below

contact_section.description: Split layout. Left column has contact details (email, phone, address) stacked vertically with labels. Right column has a large italic/bold headline, followed by a minimal form.
contact_section.form_style: Underline-only inputs (no borders, no backgrounds). Fields: Name, Email side by side, then a large textarea for project info. Submit is a text link, not a button.

buttons_and_ctas.description: This design avoids traditional filled buttons entirely. CTAs are text links with a subtle underline and an arrow icon (↗). The underline is typically in the accent blue color.
buttons_and_ctas.arrow_icon: A small northeast-pointing arrow (↗) placed after the text
buttons_and_ctas.hover_behavior: Underline expands or arrow moves slightly on hover

blog_card.structure: Image on top (no border-radius or very subtle 4px-8px), date label below, then title
blog_card.hover: subtle scale transform on image (1.02-1.03) or opacity shift

form_fields.border: none — only a bottom border of 1px solid #CCCCCC
form_fields.focus_state: bottom border transitions to #0A0A0A or accent blue
form_fields.label_style: placeholder doubles as label (placeholder shown inside field)
form_fields.textarea: same underline style, taller, with resize handle

brand_mark.color: white on accent blue circular background
brand_mark.usage: Placed next to major headlines or CTAs as a visual anchor. Never overused — appears 2-3 times max per page.

dividers.usage: Between list items (article rows). Never between major sections — whitespace handles that separation.

imagery.style: High-contrast, moody, often dark or desaturated. Architectural and abstract photography. Minimal color in images — lots of blacks, grays, and whites.

treatment.blog_thumbnails: Square or landscape crops with no border-radius or very subtle radius (4px). No overlays or filters applied via CSS.
treatment.featured_images: Large, immersive. Often dark/moody with a single human figure for scale, or abstract geometric shapes.

imagery.color_palette_of_images: Predominantly monochrome or desaturated. Occasional warm tones (orange clouds, green foliage) but always muted. Never bright or saturated stock photography.
imagery.ai_generation_guidance: If generating images for this system, prompt for: architectural photography, abstract 3D renders, moody lighting, single focal point, lots of negative space, dark backgrounds with subtle light sources, minimalist compositions.

animations_and_interactions.philosophy: Subtle and refined. Nothing flashy or attention-grabbing. Animations should feel like natural physics, not decorative.

suggested_transitions.page_elements: fade-in on scroll, slight upward translate (20px), duration 0.5-0.7s, ease-out
suggested_transitions.links: underline width transition on hover, 0.2-0.3s ease
suggested_transitions.buttons: arrow icon translate-x on hover (4px right), 0.2s ease

animations_and_interactions.loading: No spinners or skeleton screens visible in design — implies fast loading. If needed, use subtle fade-in.

responsive_behavior.approach: Desktop-first but fluid. Typography scales with viewport using clamp(). Layouts shift from multi-column to single-column below tablet breakpoints.

mobile_adjustments.typography: Headlines scale down but remain bold and dominant. Minimum hero headline size: ~36px.
mobile_adjustments.layout: All split layouts collapse to single column. Text stacks above images.
mobile_adjustments.grid: Blog cards stack vertically. Article list remains horizontal rows.

do: Use massive whitespace between sections — when it feels like too much, it's just right
Let typography be the hero — headlines should be the first thing the eye hits
Keep accent color usage to 5% or less of the total visual weight
Use moody, high-contrast imagery with minimal color
Make CTAs text-links with arrows, not chunky buttons
Constrain text blocks to ~640px max width for readability
Use asymmetric layouts (35/65 splits) over centered designs
Keep forms minimal — underline-only inputs, no backgrounds or borders
Use sentence-case for headlines, never all-caps for display text
Maintain tight letter-spacing on headlines (-0.02em to -0.03em)
dont: Don't use filled/solid buttons — this design relies on text-link CTAs
Don't use bright, saturated colors anywhere — even images should be muted
Don't add drop shadows, glows, or gradients to cards or sections
Don't use decorative borders, rounded corners larger than 8px, or badge-style elements
Don't center-align body text — always left-aligned
Don't use icons extensively — the only recurring icon is the brand star mark and the arrow (↗)
Don't crowd sections together — whitespace is a core design element
Don't use stock photography with bright colors, smiling people, or busy compositions
Don't use more than 2 font weights on a single page section (typically 400 and 700)
Don't add background colors to sections — keep everything on white or near-white

code_implementation_notes.css_approach: Use CSS custom properties (variables) for all colors and spacing values. Prefer CSS Grid for page layouts and Flexbox for component-level alignment.
code_implementation_notes.html_semantics: Use semantic HTML5: <section>, <article>, <header>, <blockquote>, <figure>, <figcaption>. Each major page section should be a <section> with generous padding.
code_implementation_notes.accessibility: Ensure sufficient color contrast (the secondary text #6B6B6B on white passes AA for large text). Form fields need associated <label> elements even if visually hidden.
